prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(declare_module user_service)
(declare_module web_client)
(declare_module core_component)
(declare_module integration_component)
(allocate_interface_to_module user_service api_interface_v1)
(assign_responsibility user_service authentication_responsibility)
(validate_responsibility user_service authentication_responsibility)
(apply_rationale_to_service_context user_service staging_context design_rationale_scalability)
(synchronize_service_context_and_rationale user_service staging_context authentication_responsibility design_rationale_scalability)
(release_responsibility user_service authentication_responsibility)
(allocate_interface_to_module web_client admin_interface)
(assign_responsibility web_client authentication_responsibility)
(validate_responsibility web_client authentication_responsibility)
(apply_rationale_to_client_context web_client production_context design_rationale_scalability)
(synchronize_client_context_and_rationale web_client production_context authentication_responsibility design_rationale_scalability)
(assemble_deployment_artifact user_service web_client staging_context production_context app_deployment_artifact)
(release_responsibility web_client authentication_responsibility)
(assign_responsibility user_service authentication_responsibility)
(validate_deployment_artifact app_deployment_artifact user_service authentication_responsibility)
(release_responsibility user_service authentication_responsibility)
(deploy_service_artifact user_service app_deployment_artifact)
(deploy_client_artifact web_client app_deployment_artifact)
(allocate_interface_to_module core_component metrics_interface)
(assign_responsibility core_component authentication_responsibility)
(validate_responsibility core_component authentication_responsibility)
(assign_stakeholder_to_module core_component team_lead)
(attach_capability_descriptor core_component capability_scaling)
(attach_compliance_record core_component compliance_policy)
(initiate_component_review core_component review_security authentication_responsibility)
(validate_component_review core_component team_lead)
(approve_component_review core_component compliance_policy)
(finalize_review core_component)
(approve_module_for_production core_component latency_slo authentication_responsibility)
(finalize_component_deployment core_component metrics_interface latency_slo)
(release_responsibility core_component authentication_responsibility)
(unassign_stakeholder_from_module core_component team_lead)
(detach_capability_descriptor core_component capability_scaling)
(detach_compliance_record core_component compliance_policy)
(allocate_interface_to_module integration_component metrics_interface)
(assign_responsibility integration_component authentication_responsibility)
(validate_responsibility integration_component authentication_responsibility)
(assign_stakeholder_to_module integration_component team_lead)
(attach_capability_descriptor integration_component capability_scaling)
(attach_compliance_record integration_component compliance_policy)
(commit_configuration_asset_to_component integration_component logging_config app_deployment_artifact)
(stage_component_for_feature_gating integration_component logging_config app_deployment_artifact authentication_responsibility)
(start_feature_gating_primary integration_component capability_scaling team_lead logging_config app_deployment_artifact)
(enable_features_stage_1 integration_component compliance_policy logging_config app_deployment_artifact)
(finalize_component_release integration_component)
(approve_module_for_production integration_component latency_slo authentication_responsibility)
(finalize_component_deployment integration_component metrics_interface latency_slo)
(release_responsibility integration_component authentication_responsibility)
(unassign_stakeholder_from_module integration_component team_lead)
(detach_capability_descriptor integration_component capability_scaling)
(detach_compliance_record integration_component compliance_policy)
(assign_responsibility user_service authentication_responsibility)
(approve_module_for_production user_service latency_slo authentication_responsibility)
(finalize_service_deployment user_service api_interface_v1 latency_slo)
(release_responsibility user_service authentication_responsibility)
(assign_responsibility web_client authentication_responsibility)
(approve_module_for_production web_client latency_slo authentication_responsibility)
(finalize_client_deployment web_client admin_interface latency_slo)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves deploying modules: user_service (service_module), web_client (client_module), core_component and integration_component (both implementation_components). The goal is to have all four deployed.


## Examining deployment scope  
- The task involves deploying four modules: user_service (service_module), web_client (client_module), core_component, and integration_component.  
- All modules must be successfully deployed to meet the goal, with user_service and web_client serving distinct roles.  
- Core_component and integration_component are classified as implementation_components, suggesting they support the system's functionality.

## Examining Deployment Steps  
- All modules (user_service, web_client, core_component, integration_component) start undeclared; declare each to begin the process.  
- After declaration, assign interfaces to modules, noting only three interfaces (api_v1, admin, metrics) are available for four modules, suggesting sharing or reallocation may be needed.  

## Checking Interface Allocation  
- Allocate interfaces like api_v1 to user_service, admin to web_client, and metrics to core_component, leaving integration_component potentially unassigned initially.  
- Consider releasing and rebounding responsibilities, as actions like release_responsibility are available, to manage limited interface assignments.  

## Reviewing Readiness Requirements  
- Modules need ready_for_deployment status, achieved through service_packaging_flag and client_packaging_flag for user_service and web_client, plus validated deployment artifacts.  
- Components (core_component, integration_component) follow a similar path, requiring approval and finalize_component_deployment for deployment, with shared app_deployment_artifact.

## Reviewing Readiness Requirements  
- Stakeholder assignment occurs after validation, e.g., assign_stakeholder_to_module for user_service to team_lead, but it becomes unavailable afterward.  
- Unassign_stakeholder_from_module is available to free up the stakeholder for other modules if needed, supporting sequential resource management.  

## Managing Resource Constraints  
- Both user_service and web_client need service_packaging_flag and client_packaging_flag to assemble the shared app_deployment_artifact, requiring both to be ready simultaneously.  
- Limited resources like one responsibility (authentication_responsibility) and three interfaces (api_v1, admin, metrics) necessitate careful allocation, such as releasing from one module to bind to another.  

## Optimizing Deployment Sequence  
- Allocate admin_interface to web_client since api_v1 is already assigned to user_service, addressing the interface limitation.  
- Consider completing user_service deployment first to free up resources like responsibility, but assembling the artifact requires both service and client to be ready, creating interdependence.

## Preparing Component Readiness  
- Core_component needs an interface; allocate metrics_interface to core_component since it’s available.  
- Release authentication_responsibility from user_service and assign it to core_component, then validate it.  

## Attaching Component Requirements  
- Attach capability_scaling and compliance_policy to core_component after validation, consuming available resources.  
- Initiate and validate a security review for core_component, requiring stakeholder assignment to team_lead first.  

## Finalizing Component Deployment  
- After review validation and compliance attachment, approve and finalize the review for core_component to achieve ready_for_deployment.  
- Note that integration_component faces challenges, as needed resources like capability and compliance are used, and review_architecture isn’t available.  

## Exploring Alternative Paths  
- For integration_component, consider a feature gating path, which requires committing configuration assets first.  
- Preparation for integration_component needs validation first, but resource constraints may limit progress similarly to core_component.

## Approving Component Deployment  
- To approve core_component for production, require non-functional characteristics (nfc) like latency_slo and authentication_responsibility, both available.  
- Finalize deployment for core_component using metrics_interface and latency_slo, making interface and nfc resources available afterward.  

## Managing Resource Availability  
- After deployment, release authentication_responsibility and unassign stakeholder team_lead from core_component to free resources.  
- Detach capability_scaling and compliance_policy from core_component, making them available for other components.  

## Preparing Integration Component  
- Declare integration_component, allocate metrics_interface, assign authentication_responsibility, and validate it, following a similar setup to core_component.  
- Assign stakeholder team_lead to integration_component, but note review_architecture is unavailable, blocking the review path.  

## Exploring Deployment Alternatives  
- For integration_component, consider feature gating to enable features, requiring test_ready, has_compliance, and has_config bound to artifact.  
- Standard deployment artifact assembly sets bound_to_staging and bound_to_production but lacks includes_staging or includes_production, affecting readiness.

## Configuring Client and Service  
- Apply scalability rationale to web_client in production_context, ensuring authentication_responsibility aligns with design needs.  
- Assemble app_deployment_artifact for user_service and web_client, covering staging and production contexts for deployment readiness.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
